title: Exact Duplicates, Boilerplate, Similarity, Canonicals, and Change Detection · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for exact duplicates, boilerplate, similarity, canonicals, and change detection and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, network, DNS, parser, filesystem, privacy, accessibility, legal-review, scale, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for content identity and change-evidence model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chang…
objectives: Keep response-byte, decoded-source, normalized-text, extracted-record, and report hashes distinct and version their transformations.
Separate repeated navigation, templates, legal text, and page-specific main content with site-tested rules and retained raw evidence.
Use documented tokenization, fingerprints, thresholds, and labeled evaluation and report uncertain similarity without claiming semantic equality.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Change Classification
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz site-quality team handles case 2191 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate byte, source, text, record, canonical, similarity, boilerplate, and change evidence; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify transport, metadata, template, content, accessibility, structured-data, and extraction-rule changes with prior and current evidence.
